steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence sets up a contrast: certain features can create a “comforting semblance of humanity,” but a robot that looks too similar to humans can make people feel more unsettled than ______. The blank must be the opposite of unsettled — something positive or at least neutral — because the structure is “more unsettled than [better state].”

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• "indifferent": Indifferent means unaffected or uncaring — not a positive state that comforting human-like features would produce. ✗
  • "vindicated": Vindicated means proved right; has nothing to do with how a robot makes someone feel. ✗
  • "revolted": Revolted is worse than unsettled — saying “more unsettled than revolted” would flip the intended meaning. ✗
  • "soothed": Soothed is the positive state the comforting features produce. “More unsettled than soothed” correctly contrasts the two outcomes: the comforting effect vs. the unnerving one. ✓
That matches the correct answer — D is correct.
